obligation_text: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be relevant, sufficiently representative, and to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete in view of the intended purpose. They shall have the appropriate statistical properties, including, where applicable, as regards the persons or groups of persons in relation to whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used.
scoring_text: Knowledge of statistical quality requirements for AI training data, including representativeness assessment, error analysis, completeness evaluation, and demographic statistical properties relevant to intended use populations.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of statistical quality requirements for AI training data, including representativeness assessment, error analysis, completeness evaluation, and demographic statistical properties relevant to intended use populations.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and executing bias audits for AI systems, including test dataset construction, metric selection, results interpretation, and remediation recommendation.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 10(3) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.558, cosine: 0.605). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.580), AI-T-007 (AI, cosine: 0.574).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.558). Shared vocabulary: [ai]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [analysis, assessment, completeness, data, demographic, error] vs WIDAI terms [audits, bias, construction, dataset, designing, executing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.871 (P=0.859, R=0.884). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 21 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 25 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of statistical quality requirements for AI training data, including re" and "Skill in designing and executing bias audits for AI systems, including test data". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.605 ≈ STS 0.558 (gap 0.047). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [ai]. EU AI Act-only: [analysis, assessment, completeness, data, demographic, error]. WIDAI-only: [audits, bias, construction, dataset, designing, executing].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.558 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
